channel.title: Vpn on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Vpn on Pi Stack

item.title: wg-easy vs WireGuard-UI vs wg-gen-web: Best WireGuard Management Interface 2026
item.description: WireGuard has become the gold standard for VPN protocols — faster, simpler, and more secure than OpenVPN or IPsec. But managing WireGuard configurations through the command line is tedious, especially when you need to add users, rotate keys, or update peer settings.

item.title: StrongSwan vs LibreSwan vs SoftEther: Best Self-Hosted VPN Gateway 2026
item.description: When you need a self-hosted VPN gateway for site-to-site connectivity, remote access, or secure network bridging, the choice of VPN software matters. While tools like wireguard and OpenVPN dominate the consumer space, enterprise-grade deployments often require the flexibility and proven security of IPSec or multi-protocol VPN solutions.

item.title: Firezone vs Pritunl vs NetBird: Self-Hosted WireGuard VPN Management 2026
item.description:
Why Self-Host Your WireGuard VPN Infrastructure

WireGuard has established itself as the fastest, most modern VPN protocol available. Its streamlined codebase (roughly 4,000 lines compared to OpenVPN’s 100,000+) delivers better throughput with lower latency and stronger cryptographic primitives. However, raw WireGuard lacks built-in user management, access controls, SSO integration, and a management interface — all critical for running a production-grade VPN.

item.title: Headscale Complete Guide 2026: Self-Host Your Own Tailscale Server (Docker Compose Setup)
item.description:
Why Self-Host a Mesh VPN?

Modern infrastructure is distributed. You have servers in the cloud, a homelab in your garage, a laptop at a coffee shop, and maybe a Raspberry Pi monitoring your garden. Connecting all of these securely without opening firewall ports or managing WireGuard by hand is where mesh VPNs shine.
